title: Mild and Sweet Northern-Thai Anchovy Fillet Bao
description: A sweet, pillowy, kid-friendly Thai dish featuring anchovy fillet and wok-hei, adapted to be kosher-friendly.

ingredients.name: Fish sauce (optional)
ingredients.notes: if desired, use tamari for a vegan alternative

instructions: Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat until smoky.
Add 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil and swirl to coat the pan.
Cook the anchovy fillets for 30 seconds on each side, or until they release their oil and start to crisp.
Add the brown sugar to the pan and stir to combine with the anchovy oil.
Wrap the bao dough in a damp cloth and let it rest for 10 minutes.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on desired bao size.
Roll each piece into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Place a few anchovy fillets in the center of each disk, then fold the dough over the toppings to form a half-moon shape.
Seal the edges by crimping with a fork or pressing with a knife.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in the wok or skillet over medium heat.
Cook the bao for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they're golden brown and crispy.
Serve the bao hot, garnished with chopped scallions and cilantro if desired.
